description: SDK entry (createProfile / create_profile) conformance scenarios. Each scenario drives the SDK entry point as a subprocess: the test writes the listed `turn` object as a single NDJSON line to the harness's stdin, and a handler of the named `kind` runs under create_profile / createProfile. Both reference SDKs run the same fixture (Node: sdk/node/src/sdk.test.js, Python: sdk/python/tests/test_sdk.py) and assert the exact stdout NDJSON event lines and exit code, so the user-facing SDK contract cannot drift between languages. Handler kinds are implemented per-language in sdk_harness.js / _sdk_harness.py. Both SDKs accept async and sync handlers; the sync kinds pin that parity (a sync handler can return a value and can call send_partial / send_error bare). Wire 0.3: input is a turn object on stdin, output is NDJSON events on stdout (no AGENT_* env, no sentinel prefixes).

scenarios.name: async-send-error-then-return
scenarios.note: Pins that send_error is non-terminal in both SDKs — the handler may continue and return a body. The bridge decides what to do with an error event + text; the SDK does not force-exit on sendError (only on a thrown ProtocolError).

scenarios.name: sync-returns-string
scenarios.note: Pins that a plain (non-async) handler returning a string works on both SDKs. Pre-fix Python required async; now it accepts sync too, matching Node.

scenarios.name: sync-uses-send-partial-bare
scenarios.note: Pins that a sync handler can call send_partial / sendPartial without awaiting and the chunk is still emitted. Python's send_partial now writes at call time and returns a no-op awaitable so `await ctx.send_partial(...)` (async) and `ctx.send_partial(...)` (sync) both work.

scenarios.name: async-returns-partial-with-role
scenarios.note: Pins the optional `role` field on partial events across both SDKs.
